reasoning: Given the location (Troyes, France), daily average temperatures trend downward from early November (~7-8°C) into December and February (~3-4°C). The temperature on Nov 9 is expected to be higher than the climatological averages on all three future dates, meaning the unadjusted probability of a warmer day occurring later in the winter is inherently well below 0.5. However, meteorological variance combined with the strict instruction that uncertainty must increase with forecast horizon—pushing probabilities for more distant dates closer to 0.5—requires progressive smoothing of these base estimates. I've structured the predictions starting firmly below 0.5 for the 1-week horizon (where the near-term cooling trend is strongly predictive) and moved the 1-month and 3-month probabilities incrementally toward 0.5 to accurately follow the prescribed uncertainty penalty.
